title: Lesson 27 — The Skeptic

words.definition: unwilling or unable to believe something
words.example: She gave an incredulous laugh at the outlandish excuse.

words.definition: too ready to believe things; easily deceived
words.example: The scam preyed on credulous investors.

words.definition: easily persuaded to believe something; easily fooled
words.example: Only a gullible buyer would fall for that pitch.

words.definition: lacking experience, wisdom, or judgment; innocently trusting
words.example: It was naive to expect the deal to have no catch.

words.definition: believing that people are motivated purely by self-interest; distrustful
words.example: Years in politics had left him cynical about promises.

words.definition: tired, bored, or unimpressed after too much of something
words.example: Jaded critics had seen every trick the genre could offer.

words.definition: disappointed by discovering that something is not as good as one believed
words.example: Disillusioned volunteers drifted away from the campaign.

words.definition: to speak about something in a scornfully mocking way
words.example: Colleagues scoffed at the theory before the data proved it.

words.definition: to harm the reputation or believability of
words.example: The forged email was meant to discredit the witness.

words.definition: a person inclined to doubt accepted opinions or claims
words.example: A lifelong skeptic, she demanded evidence for everything.

words.definition: a humorous or malicious deception; a trick presented as truth
words.example: The photo of the sea monster turned out to be a hoax.

words.definition: a person who falsely claims special knowledge or skill; a fraud
words.example: The so-called healer was exposed as a charlatan.

story: When the traveling "psychic" came to town, the crowd was [[credulous|credulous]], but one [[skeptic|skeptic]] in the back row was frankly [[incredulous|incredulous]]. She had watched too many [[gullible|gullible]] neighbors, [[naive|naive]] and hopeful, hand over their savings. [[cynical|Cynical]] and a little [[jaded|jaded]] after years of such acts, she was already [[disillusioned|disillusioned]] before he began. She did not [[scoff|scoff]] aloud; instead she quietly gathered the proof that would [[discredit|discredit]] him, revealing the whole performance as a [[hoax|hoax]] and its star as a common [[charlatan|charlatan]].
